story.iWant: to create new templates via `/sb:add-template` with conversational discovery
story.soThat: I can expand my template library for specific use cases

tasks.task: Implement warm welcome and opening question
Implement deep discovery questions (content type, elements, style, examples)
Implement frontend-design skill invocation with full context
Implement template file save to catalog/
Implement catalog.json update with new entry
Test workflow with various template types

criterion.description: Given I run `/sb:add-template`, when the workflow starts, then I'm warmly welcomed and asked "What kind of slide template do you need?"

Given I describe a template need, when the agent explores my requirements, then it asks about: Content type (data visualization, text-heavy, mixed media), Visual elements needed (charts, diagrams, icons, columns, images), Visual style preferences (matches theme personality), Reference examples or inspiration (if available), Specific use cases (when would this template be used)

Given discovery is complete (minimum 3-5 exchanges), when I confirm the spec summary, then: Template ID is generated (kebab-case from template name), HTML template is generated via frontend-design skill, Template file is saved to `config/catalog/{id}.html`, catalog.json is updated with new entry, `source` is set to "add-template", `created_at` is set to current timestamp

Given template generation completes, when the workflow finishes, then: Success message shows template ID and file path, User is offered to open template in browser for preview, catalog.json shows incremented template count

doc.title: Technical Specification: Template Catalog System
doc.snippet: Ask: What kind of slide do you need? Explore: What content will it display? Clarify: Visual style preferences? Confirm: Summarize template spec. Generate: Use frontend-design skill. Save: Add to catalog.json and save HTML. Verify: Offer browser preview.

doc.title: Technical Specification: Template Catalog System
doc.snippet: Template entries require: id (kebab-case slug), name (human-readable), description (purpose), use_cases (array of keywords), file (filename.html), preview (null), created_at (ISO timestamp), source ("setup" or "add-template").

doc.title: Epic Breakdown - Epic 11
doc.snippet: Create new workflow for on-demand template creation. Emphasizes deep conversational discovery (3-5 exchanges minimum) to understand user needs before generating. Uses frontend-design skill for HTML generation.

doc.title: Slide Builder Conventions
doc.snippet: To add a new command: 1) Create workflow directory .slide-builder/workflows/{command-name}/, 2) Add workflow.yaml with BMAD schema, 3) Add instructions.md with workflow steps, 4) Create skill registration .claude/commands/sb/{command-name}.md, 5) Update CONVENTIONS.md with mapping.

doc.title: Slide Builder Conventions
doc.snippet: Catalog location at .slide-builder/config/catalog/ with catalog.json manifest and *.html template files. Template IDs use kebab-case slugs. Source field tracks origin: "setup" for initial, "add-template" for user-created.

doc.title: Slide Builder Conventions
doc.snippet: Required fields: name, description, author, installed_path, instructions. Optional: variables, template (false for non-document), standalone (true). Path patterns use {project-root} and {installed_path} variables.

artifact.reason: Reference pattern for workflow.yaml structure - includes config_path, catalog_path, catalog_manifest, required_skills, template:false, standalone:true
Reference pattern for invoking frontend-design skill with theme context, slide requirements, and technical requirements. Copy this invocation pattern for template generation.
Reference pattern for skill registration - frontmatter with description, steps to read workflow.yaml and instructions.md, execute steps in order
Existing catalog.json with 6 templates - add-template workflow must read, append new entry, and write back. Preserve existing entries.
Full theme context required for frontend-design skill invocation. Pass colors, typography, shapes, personality for brand consistency.
Reference for generated template structure - CSS variables, contenteditable attributes, data-field attributes, 1920x1080 dimensions

ecosystem.name: node
ecosystem.package: Headless browser - not used for this story
Google API - not used for this story

internal.dependency: Core dependency - used to generate HTML template based on discovered requirements
Existing manifest to append new template entry
Brand theme for template generation consistency

constraints.constraint: Generated templates MUST use CSS custom properties from theme.json (--color-primary, --font-heading, etc.)
Template ID must be kebab-case slug generated from user-provided template name
Template HTML filename must match ID: {id}.html
Catalog entry source field must be "add-template" (not "setup")
Workflow YAML must follow BMAD schema with required fields: name, description, installed_path, instructions
All text elements must have contenteditable="true" and unique data-field attribute
Slides must be exactly 1920x1080 pixels with viewport meta tag
Minimum 3-5 conversational exchanges before generating template
Discovery must ask about: content type, visual elements, style preferences, reference examples, use cases
Skill registration file must be markdown in .claude/commands/sb/{name}.md

interface.name: catalog.json append operation
interface.signature: Read catalog.json → Parse templates array → Append new entry → Update lastModified → Write back

interface.name: New template entry schema
interface.signature: {id: "kebab-case-slug", name: "User Template Name", description: "Generated description", use_cases: ["keyword1", "keyword2"], file: "{id}.html", preview: null, created_at: "ISO-timestamp", source: "add-template"}

interface.name: frontend-design skill invocation
interface.signature: Skill tool with skill: "frontend-design", prompt includes: slide intent, content requirements, visual elements, theme colors/typography/personality, technical requirements (1920x1080, contenteditable, data-field, CSS variables)

interface.name: workflow.yaml required structure
interface.signature: name: string, description: string, author: string, installed_path: "{project-root}/.slide-builder/workflows/add-template", instructions: "{installed_path}/instructions.md", config_path, catalog_path, catalog_manifest, theme_file, template: false, standalone: true

interface.name: skill registration structure
interface.signature: Frontmatter with description, body with steps: 1) Read workflow.yaml, 2) Read instructions.md, 3) Execute steps in order, 4) Update status.yaml

tests.standards: No automated test framework exists. Manual validation via running /sb:add-template workflow and verifying: 1) Conversational discovery asks required questions, 2) Template HTML is generated correctly, 3) catalog.json is updated with new entry, 4) Generated template follows theme and technical requirements.

locations.location: JSON validation of catalog.json after workflow
Browser preview of generated template at 1920x1080

ideas.idea: Run /sb:add-template, verify warm welcome message and opening question appears
Describe a template need, verify workflow asks about content type, visual elements, style, examples, use cases across multiple exchanges
Verify at least 3 conversational exchanges before generation begins
After confirming spec, verify HTML file created at config/catalog/{id}.html
Read catalog.json, verify new entry has id, name, description, use_cases, file, source="add-template", created_at timestamp
Verify success message shows template ID and file path
Verify browser preview is offered after generation
Verify catalog.json template count increased by 1
Open generated template in browser, verify 1920x1080 dimensions, CSS variables from theme, contenteditable text elements

file.change: Create workflow directory for add-template skill
Create workflow configuration with: name, description, author, paths (config_path, catalog_path, catalog_manifest, theme_file), instructions path, template:false, standalone:true

Create instructions with conversational discovery flow: welcome, open question, content exploration, visual elements, style preferences, reference check, use case clarity, spec confirmation, frontend-design invocation, save template, update catalog.json, preview offer
Create skill registration file with frontmatter description and execution steps
At runtime: Append new template entry to templates array, update lastModified timestamp

learnings.learning: Catalog directory established with manifest schema. Template IDs use kebab-case, ISO 8601 timestamps.
Setup workflow now generates templates to catalog/ with ID-based filenames. catalog.json populated with 6 starter templates. source="setup" for initial templates.
Catalog.json is now fully populated and can be appended to. Template generation pattern via frontend-design skill is established.

conversationalFlowReference.phase: Greet user, explain the add-template process briefly, set collaborative tone
"What kind of slide template do you need?" - Let user describe in their own words
"What will this slide typically display?" - Understand the information architecture
"What specific elements would you like?" - Charts, diagrams, icons, columns, images
"How should it feel visually?" - Match theme personality (bold, minimal, technical)
"Do you have any examples or inspiration?" - Optional visual references
"When would you use this template?" - Generate use_cases array
Summarize template requirements, ask for approval before generation
Invoke frontend-design skill with full context
Write HTML to catalog/{id}.html, update catalog.json
"Would you like to open the template in your browser?"
